description: Add inter-level review agent that checks completed work and creates fix stories if needed

userStories.title: Add review event types to events.rs
userStories.description: Add three new variants to the BaroEvent enum in crates/baro-tui/src/events.rs: ReviewStart { level: usize }, ReviewLog { line: String }, ReviewComplete { level: usize, passed: bool, fix_count: u32 }. Each variant needs a serde rename attribute matching the snake_case pattern used by existing variants (e.g. #[serde(rename = "review_start")]). Keep the existing Deserialize, Clone, Debug derives working.

userStories.title: Add git commit hash helper to git.rs
userStories.description: Add a public async function get_commit_hash(cwd: &Path) -> Result<String, String> to crates/baro-tui/src/git.rs that runs 'git rev-parse HEAD' and returns the trimmed commit hash string. Follow the same pattern as the existing get_current_branch function. Make it pub(crate).

userStories.title: Add review tracking state to App
userStories.description: In crates/baro-tui/src/app.rs: 1) Add three fields to the App struct: review_in_progress: bool, review_level: usize, review_logs: Vec<String>. 2) Initialize them in App::new() (false, 0, empty vec). 3) In handle_event(), add match arms for the three new BaroEvent variants: ReviewStart sets review_in_progress=true and review_level=level. ReviewLog appends line to review_logs (cap at MAX_LOG_LINES). ReviewComplete sets review_in_progress=false.

userStories.title: Implement review agent logic in executor.rs
userStories.description: In crates/baro-tui/src/executor.rs, add a review step in run_executor() that runs after each level completes. Implementation: 1) Before the level's story loop, save the commit hash using crate::git::get_commit_hash(&cwd). 2) After all handles for a level are collected, run a review function. 3) The review function: a) Emits ReviewStart { level: level_index }. b) Runs 'git diff SAVED_HASH HEAD' to get the diff. c) Collects acceptance criteria from all completed stories in this level. d) Spawns 'claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --output-format json -p' with a prompt including the diff and acceptance criteria asking for JSON { passed: boolean, fixes: [{ title: string, description: string }] }. e) Parses the JSON response. If passed=true, emits ReviewComplete { level, passed: true, fix_count: 0 }. f) If passed=false, creates new PrdStory entries for each fix with ids like S{N}-fix1, S{N}-fix2 where N is the first story of the level. Executes them as regular stories (they get committed and pushed). Then re-reviews with a new diff from SAVED_HASH to HEAD. g) Maximum 2 review-fix cycles per level. If still failing after 2 rounds, log a warning and emit ReviewComplete with passed=false and continue. Throughout, emit ReviewLog events for status updates. Handle errors gracefully - if review agent fails to parse JSON or claude fails, log warning and continue to next level.

userStories.title: Add review status display to TUI dashboard
userStories.description: In crates/baro-tui/src/screens/execute_dashboard.rs: 1) In the story list panel (render_story_list), after rendering all stories in a level, if app.review_in_progress is true and app.review_level matches the current level index, add a line like 'Reviewing Level N...' in cyan with a spinner character (use app.tick_count to rotate through spinner chars like ['⠋','⠙','⠹','⠸','⠼','⠴','⠦','⠧','⠇','⠏']). 2) In the logs panel (render_logs), when no active stories exist but review_in_progress is true, show the review_logs from app.review_logs instead of the 'Waiting for next story...' message. Use a 'Review' title for the log block.

userStories.title: Final build verification - zero warnings
userStories.description: Run cargo build for the entire workspace and fix any warnings or errors. Check for unused imports, unused variables, dead code warnings, etc. Ensure cargo build completes with zero warnings and zero errors. Do NOT add #[allow(dead_code)] or similar suppression attributes - fix the actual issues instead. Read every file that was modified by previous stories (events.rs, git.rs, app.rs, executor.rs, execute_dashboard.rs) and verify correctness.
